subject: Ecology
title: Mountain Cloud Capture
description: A micro-lesson that makes Mountain Cloud Capture usable.

hook.text: Mountain forests intercept fog and low clouds, extracting water that rain alone can't provide.

buildup.text: Tiny droplets collect on needles and leaves, then drip to the ground—fog drip.

discovery.text: In some mountain regions, fog drip doubles the effective rainfall a forest receives.

twist.text: Cut the forest and fog passes straight through. Streams dry up even if rain stays the same.

climax.text: Mountain forests supply drinking water for hundreds of millions of people downstream.

punchline.text: Mountain trees catch what rain misses.

quiz.question: What is fog drip?
options: Water collected from fog on tree surfaces
Dew that forms on the ground
